brief.headline: Portfolio value at stake reaches 21,181,431.68 AED across 280 flagged SKUs — immediate action required on cash release, expiry avoidance, and stockout replenishment
brief.body_markdown:
## Portfolio Value-at-Stake — Executive Summary

**Total value at stake: 21,181,431.68 AED**
This figure spans 280 flagged SKUs out of a portfolio of 600. It is an upper bound across three distinct dimensions — releasable cash, write-off exposure, and stockout margin loss — not a simple additive sum. A SKU contributing to more than one dimension is counted in each relevant measure, so the total overstates the independently actionable pool.

---

### First Priority — Release Trapped Working Capital

The largest opportunity is the slow-excess cluster, where 16,481,192.86 AED is recoverable across 173 SKUs, all carrying `safe_to_release = true` and sitting materially above target coverage levels. Stock-return, liquidation, or MOQ renegotiation actions can be initiated immediately with no service-level risk. Freed cash is earmarked to fund the emergency replenishment required by the stockout cluster below.

---

### Second Priority — Expiry Write-Off Avoidance

The expiry cluster presents a write-off exposure of 280,657.13 AED across 30 near-expiry SKUs. Accelerated sell-through and supplier return programmes must be activated without delay; the most urgent batches carry the shortest remaining shelf life. Failure to act converts this exposure directly into a P&L charge.

---

### Third Priority — Stockout Margin Protection

The stockout cluster carries 4,419,581.69 AED of at-risk contribution margin across 84 SKUs that are at or below their reorder points. Releasing inventory from this cluster would breach service-level targets; the required action is emergency replenishment, funded by the cash freed from the slow-excess release. Without intervention, this margin loss will crystallise as sales are lost to out-of-stock conditions.

---

### Recommended Immediate Actions

| Priority | Cluster | Value at Stake (AED) | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| First | Slow Excess | 16,481,192.86 | Release excess stock; recover working capital |
| Second | Near-Expiry | 280,657.13 | Accelerate sell-through; initiate supplier returns |
| Third | Stockout | 4,419,581.69 | Emergency replenishment; fund from released cash |

The board is asked to authorise immediate execution of the first and second priority actions and to approve emergency purchase orders for the stockout cluster, to be funded from the working capital released by the slow-excess programme.
